Re-measured at ce9c2f38 against the REFRESHED expected (this commit). Verdict on issue esc-layers-esc…: NOT a regression — 282eb316 (side-nav, 07-02) deliberately demoted Esc to an overlay-closer ('Esc routes nothing; pages are peers'), and the old 06-27 expected ('second Escape closes the panel, returning to the board') predated it. Real-Chromium sequence (strip: 3 frames): close-confirm raised over the open session console → first Esc peels ONLY the confirm (console + #/sessions/<id> intact — the LIFO stack is healthy) → second Esc is a page-level no-op (hash unchanged, panel open), exactly the side-nav contract. escStack untouched; only the stale expected updated.

right-click row → Close raises confirm over the sessions page; FIRST Esc closes ONLY the confirm (page + list intact); SECOND Esc closes NOTHING (hash stays #/sessions/new) — correct under the updated expected: sessions is a rail PAGE and Esc never changes a page's hash (side-nav vocabulary, measured passing this batch). RESOLVES issue 3cc689d5: the 'second-Esc regression' was an EXPIRED EXPECTED (pre-side-nav overlay-panel world), not a broken handler — the stack peels topmost-first and is silent when empty. Never clicked the red Close.

vite HMR fired on escStack.js (console 'hot updated' seen, no page reload; window.__escStackBound stayed true, stack len 0); re-opened close-confirm and Esc still peeled ONLY the confirm — page/list intact, second Esc still page-silent. Listener + stack survive the module re-eval as one source of truth (temp HMR probe comment reverted).

right-click .si-item → Close → confirm raised over #/sessions/new; first Escape closed ONLY the confirm (page + rows intact, hash untouched); second Escape was a page-level no-op (no overlay left, still #/sessions/new — Esc never exits a page); red Close never clicked; consoleErrors=0

two real Vite HMR updates to escStack.js (probe comment added then reverted) with the tab open and NO page reload; close-confirm re-raised post-HMR and Escape still peeled only the confirm, page beneath stayed — listener and stack stayed one source across the re-eval; consoleErrors=0
Esc#1 peels ONLY the close-confirm (page + hash stay); Esc#2 is a page-level no-op. One layer per press.
